dag.dag_name: Warehouse pick-wave generation Kubernetes pod queue congestion

tasks.dependencies: Fetches the Ecommerce entity list (stores/zones/segments/vendors) to fan out over
One mapped instance per entity, bound to a dedicated Airflow pool
Runs in parallel with process_unit, watches shared pool capacity
Fan-in of all mapped instances plus the monitor

operator_config.sql: SELECT 1 AS autorca_probe, 'RTL_ECM_QUEUE' AS dag_id, 'load_target' AS task_name;

tasks.dependencies: Evaluates whether queue congestion impacted this or sibling DAGs

incident.incident_name: Warehouse pick-wave generation Kubernetes pod queue congestion
incident.description: Queue Congestion incident on the Ecommerce pipeline (RTL_ECM_QUEUE): warehouse pick-wave generation Kubernetes pod queue congestion.
incident.symptoms: Dynamically mapped pick-wave generation tasks (one per zone, 35 zones) queued for pod scheduling for over an hour.
incident.root_cause: The Kubernetes namespace's resource quota (CPU requests) was sized for the DAG's original 10-zone design and never updated after zone count grew to 35, so the scheduler could only start 10 pods concurrently, queuing the rest.
incident.business_impact: Pick-wave release to the warehouse floor delayed by over an hour during peak shift, directly reducing units-picked-per-hour for that shift.
incident.resolution: Increase the namespace's CPU/memory resource quota to match current zone count; add a pre-deployment check that validates quota sufficiency against the DAG's declared max concurrency.

expected_rca.expected_rca_summary: AI AutoRCA should identify: The Kubernetes namespace's resource quota (CPU requests) was sized for the DAG's original 10-zone design and never updated after zone count grew to 35, so the scheduler could only start 10 pods concurrently, queuing the rest.
expected_rca.evidence_required: Airflow task logs, Datadog infrastructure/application metrics, JIRA/Confluence RAG context for known-issue matching
expected_rca.airflow_logs: Airflow task instances show 'queued' state with Kubernetes scheduler events indicating 'Insufficient cpu'
expected_rca.datadog_metrics: Datadog kubernetes.namespace.cpu_requests hit the quota ceiling exactly when queued pod count began climbing
expected_rca.resolution: Increase the namespace's CPU/memory resource quota to match current zone count; add a pre-deployment check that validates quota sufficiency against the DAG's declared max concurrency.
expected_rca.preventive_action: Add expected-concurrent-pod-count as a required metadata field so infrastructure capacity planning is validated automatically whenever a DAG's mapping cardinality changes.
